 Wic-approved Foods: A Complete List
WIC-Approved Foods: A Complete List
Are you enrolled in the
WIC program
or considering applying? The
Women, Infants, and Children (WIC)
program provides access to
nutritious foods for low-income families
,
helping support the health of pregnant women, new moms, and
children
under the age of five. One of the most common questions is:
What foods are WIC-approved?
In this article, we break down the
WIC-approved food list
, highlight key categories, and help you navigate your next grocery trip with confidence.
What Is the WIC Program?
The
WIC program
is a federally funded nutrition assistance initiative offering
healthy food benefits
, nutrition education,
breastfeeding support
,
and healthcare referrals to eligible participants. One of the major benefits of WIC is access to
WIC-approved groceries
that support a balanced diet for mothers and young children.
WIC-Approved Foods: A Complete List by Category
While the specific food brands and sizes may vary slightly by state, the core categories of
WIC-eligible foods
remain consistent. Here’s a breakdown:
1. Fruits and Vegetables
Fresh, frozen, or canned options
100% fruit or vegetable juice (specific brands and sizes apply)
Organic options may be eligible
2.
Whole Grains
100% whole wheat bread
Brown rice
Whole wheat tortillas
Oatmeal and barley
Corn tortillas (WIC-approved brands only)
3. Milk and Dairy Alternatives
Low-fat or non-fat milk (whole milk for children under 2)
Lactose-free milk
Soy milk (with calcium and vitamin D)
Cheese (block or shredded, plain only)
Yogurt (fat-free or low-fat, no artificial sweeteners)
4. Infant Foods
Infant formula (specific brands only)
Infant cereal
Jarred baby food (fruits, vegetables, meats)
No added sugar, salt, or starch
5. Cereal
Iron-fortified cereals
Cold and hot cereals
Low in sugar (specific grams per serving required)
6. Protein-Rich Foods
Eggs
Canned beans (black beans, kidney beans, lentils, etc.)
Dry beans and peas
Peanut butter
Canned tuna or salmon (for breastfeeding moms only)
7. Juice (For Women and Children Over One)
100% fruit juice only
No added sugar
Approved brands in limited quantities (usually 48 oz or smaller)
Helpful Tips for Shopping with WIC
Check your state’s WIC food guide
– Different states may approve different brands or sizes.
Download your WIC app
– Many states have apps that let you scan barcodes and check if an item is eligible.
Look for WIC labels
in the grocery store aisles to find approved items faster.
Ask store staff
if you’re unsure – Most grocery chains train employees to help WIC shoppers.
Using your
WIC food benefits
can be a great way to ensure your family receives
nutritious, balanced meals
while keeping costs low. With this complete list of
WIC-approved foods
, you can shop smarter and healthier.
